// <auto-generated>This file has been auto generated from 'Qyl.OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions.SourceGeneration'.</auto-generated>

#nullable enable

#pragma warning disable CS1570 // XML comment has badly formed XML

namespace OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions;

/// <summary>
/// Typed setter extensions for OpenTelemetry semantic-convention attributes on a span.
/// Each method invokes <c>global::System.Diagnostics.Activity.SetTag</c> with the
/// registry-defined key and a strongly-typed value.
/// </summary>
static partial class HttpActivityExtensions
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `client.address` instead.
    /// </summary>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by client.address.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpClientIp(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.client_ip", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `network.protocol.name` and `network.protocol.version` instead.
    /// </summary>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Split into `network.protocol.name` and `network.protocol.version`")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpFlavor(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.flavor", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use one of `server.address`, `client.address` or `http.request.header.host` instead, depending on the usage.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - www.example.org
    /// </remarks>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by one of `server.address`, `client.address` or `http.request.header.host`, depending on the usage. ")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpHost(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.host", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `http.request.method` instead.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - GET
    /// - POST
    /// - HEAD
    /// </remarks>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by http.request.method.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpMethod(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.method", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// HTTP request headers, `<key>` being the normalized HTTP Header name (lowercase), the value being the header values.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which headers are to be captured.
    /// Including all request headers can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.
    ///
    /// The `User-Agent` header is already captured in the `user_agent.original` attribute.
    /// Users MAY explicitly configure instrumentations to capture them even though it is not recommended.
    ///
    /// The attribute value MUST consist of either multiple header values as an array of strings
    /// or a single-item array containing a possibly comma-concatenated string, depending on the way
    /// the HTTP library provides access to headers.
    ///
    /// Examples:
    ///
    /// - A header `Content-Type: application/json` SHOULD be recorded as the `http.request.header.content-type`
    ///   attribute with value `["application/json"]`.
    /// - A header `X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4, 1.2.3.5` SHOULD be recorded as the `http.request.header.x-forwarded-for`
    ///   attribute with value `["1.2.3.4", "1.2.3.5"]` or `["1.2.3.4, 1.2.3.5"]` depending on the HTTP library.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - ["application/json"]
    /// - ["1.2.3.4","1.2.3.5"]
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpRequestHeader(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string segment,
        string[] value)
        => activity.SetTag($"http.request.header.{segment}", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// HTTP request method.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// HTTP request method value SHOULD be "known" to the instrumentation.
    /// By default, this convention defines "known" methods as the ones listed in [RFC9110](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-methods),
    /// the PATCH method defined in [RFC5789](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5789.html)
    /// and the QUERY method defined in [httpbis-safe-method-w-body](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-httpbis-safe-method-w-body/?include_text=1).
    ///
    /// If the HTTP request method is not known to instrumentation, it MUST set the `http.request.method` attribute to `_OTHER`.
    ///
    /// If the HTTP instrumentation could end up converting valid HTTP request methods to `_OTHER`, then it MUST provide a way to override
    /// the list of known HTTP methods. If this override is done via environment variable, then the environment variable MUST be named
    /// OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_KNOWN_METHODS and support a comma-separated list of case-sensitive known HTTP methods.
    ///
    /// ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue)
    /// If this override is done via declarative configuration, then the list MUST be configurable via the `known_methods` property
    /// (an array of case-sensitive strings with minimum items 0) under `.instrumentation/development.general.http.client` and/or
    /// `.instrumentation/development.general.http.server`.
    ///
    /// In either case, this list MUST be a full override of the default known methods,
    /// it is not a list of known methods in addition to the defaults.
    ///
    /// HTTP method names are case-sensitive and `http.request.method` attribute value MUST match a known HTTP method name exactly.
    /// Instrumentations for specific web frameworks that consider HTTP methods to be case insensitive, SHOULD populate a canonical equivalent.
    /// Tracing instrumentations that do so, MUST also set `http.request.method_original` to the original value.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - GET
    /// - POST
    /// - HEAD
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpRequestMethod(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.request.method", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Original HTTP method sent by the client in the request line.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - GeT
    /// - ACL
    /// - foo
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpRequestMethodOriginal(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.request.method_original", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// The ordinal number of request resending attempt (for any reason, including redirects).
    ///
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// The resend count SHOULD be updated each time an HTTP request gets resent by the client, regardless of what was the cause of the resending (e.g. redirection, authorization failure, 503 Server Unavailable, network issues, or any other).
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpRequestResendCount(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        long value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.request.resend_count", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `http.request.header.content-length` instead.
    /// </summary>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by `http.request.header.content-length`.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpRequestContentLength(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        long value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.request_content_length", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `http.request.body.size` instead.
    /// </summary>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by http.request.body.size.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpRequestContentLengthUncompressed(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        long value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.request_content_length_uncompressed", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// HTTP response headers, `<key>` being the normalized HTTP Header name (lowercase), the value being the header values.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which headers are to be captured.
    /// Including all response headers can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.
    ///
    /// Users MAY explicitly configure instrumentations to capture them even though it is not recommended.
    ///
    /// The attribute value MUST consist of either multiple header values as an array of strings
    /// or a single-item array containing a possibly comma-concatenated string, depending on the way
    /// the HTTP library provides access to headers.
    ///
    /// Examples:
    ///
    /// - A header `Content-Type: application/json` header SHOULD be recorded as the `http.request.response.content-type`
    ///   attribute with value `["application/json"]`.
    /// - A header `My-custom-header: abc, def` header SHOULD be recorded as the `http.response.header.my-custom-header`
    ///   attribute with value `["abc", "def"]` or `["abc, def"]` depending on the HTTP library.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - ["application/json"]
    /// - ["abc","def"]
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpResponseHeader(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string segment,
        string[] value)
        => activity.SetTag($"http.response.header.{segment}", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// [HTTP response status code](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6).
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - 200
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpResponseStatusCode(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        long value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.response.status_code", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `http.response.header.content-length` instead.
    /// </summary>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by `http.response.header.content-length`.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpResponseContentLength(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        long value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.response_content_length", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `http.response.body.size` instead.
    /// </summary>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by http.response.body.size.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpResponseContentLengthUncompressed(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        long value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.response_content_length_uncompressed", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// The matched route template for the request. This MUST be low-cardinality and include all static path segments, with dynamic path segments represented with placeholders.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// MUST NOT be populated when this is not supported by the HTTP server framework as the route attribute should have low-cardinality and the URI path can NOT substitute it.
    /// SHOULD include the [application root](/docs/http/http-spans.md#http-server-definitions) if there is one.
    ///
    /// A static path segment is a part of the route template with a fixed, low-cardinality value. This includes literal strings like `/users/` and placeholders that
    /// are constrained to a finite, predefined set of values, e.g. `{controller}` or `{action}`.
    ///
    /// A dynamic path segment is a placeholder for a value that can have high cardinality and is not constrained to a predefined list like static path segments.
    ///
    /// Instrumentations SHOULD use routing information provided by the corresponding web framework. They SHOULD pick the most precise source of routing information and MAY
    /// support custom route formatting. Instrumentations SHOULD document the format and the API used to obtain the route string.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - /users/:userID?
    /// - my-controller/my-action/{id?}
    /// </remarks>
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpRoute(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.route", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `url.scheme` instead.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - http
    /// - https
    /// </remarks>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by url.scheme.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpScheme(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.scheme", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `server.address` instead.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - example.com
    /// </remarks>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by server.address.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpServerName(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.server_name", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `http.response.status_code` instead.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - 200
    /// </remarks>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by http.response.status_code.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpStatusCode(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        long value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.status_code", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `url.path` and `url.query` instead.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - /search?q=OpenTelemetry#SemConv
    /// </remarks>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Removed, no replacement.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpTarget(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.target", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `url.full` instead.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - https://www.foo.bar/search?q=OpenTelemetry#SemConv
    /// </remarks>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by url.full.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpUrl(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.url", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `user_agent.original` instead.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - CERN-LineMode/2.15 libwww/2.17b3
    /// - Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 14_7_1 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/14.1.2 Mobile/15E148 Safari/604.1
    /// </remarks>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Replaced by user_agent.original.")]
    public static global::System.Diagnostics.Activity SetHttpUserAgent(
        this global::System.Diagnostics.Activity activity,
        string value)
        => activity.SetTag("http.user_agent", value);

    /// <summary>
    /// Deprecated, use `network.protocol.name` and `network.protocol.version` instead.
    /// </summary>
    [global::System.Obsolete("Split into `network.protocol.name` and `network.protocol.version`")]
    public static class HttpFlavorValues
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// HTTP/1.0
        /// </summary>
        public const string Http10 = "1.0";

        /// <summary>
        /// HTTP/1.1
        /// </summary>
        public const string Http11 = "1.1";

        /// <summary>
        /// HTTP/2
        /// </summary>
        public const string Http20 = "2.0";

        /// <summary>
        /// HTTP/3
        /// </summary>
        public const string Http30 = "3.0";

        /// <summary>
        /// SPDY protocol.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Spdy = "SPDY";

        /// <summary>
        /// QUIC protocol.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Quic = "QUIC";
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// HTTP request method.
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// HTTP request method value SHOULD be "known" to the instrumentation.
    /// By default, this convention defines "known" methods as the ones listed in [RFC9110](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-methods),
    /// the PATCH method defined in [RFC5789](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5789.html)
    /// and the QUERY method defined in [httpbis-safe-method-w-body](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-httpbis-safe-method-w-body/?include_text=1).
    ///
    /// If the HTTP request method is not known to instrumentation, it MUST set the `http.request.method` attribute to `_OTHER`.
    ///
    /// If the HTTP instrumentation could end up converting valid HTTP request methods to `_OTHER`, then it MUST provide a way to override
    /// the list of known HTTP methods. If this override is done via environment variable, then the environment variable MUST be named
    /// OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_KNOWN_METHODS and support a comma-separated list of case-sensitive known HTTP methods.
    ///
    /// ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue)
    /// If this override is done via declarative configuration, then the list MUST be configurable via the `known_methods` property
    /// (an array of case-sensitive strings with minimum items 0) under `.instrumentation/development.general.http.client` and/or
    /// `.instrumentation/development.general.http.server`.
    ///
    /// In either case, this list MUST be a full override of the default known methods,
    /// it is not a list of known methods in addition to the defaults.
    ///
    /// HTTP method names are case-sensitive and `http.request.method` attribute value MUST match a known HTTP method name exactly.
    /// Instrumentations for specific web frameworks that consider HTTP methods to be case insensitive, SHOULD populate a canonical equivalent.
    /// Tracing instrumentations that do so, MUST also set `http.request.method_original` to the original value.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Examples:
    /// - GET
    /// - POST
    /// - HEAD
    /// </remarks>
    public static class HttpRequestMethodValues
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// CONNECT method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Connect = "CONNECT";

        /// <summary>
        /// DELETE method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Delete = "DELETE";

        /// <summary>
        /// GET method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Get = "GET";

        /// <summary>
        /// HEAD method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Head = "HEAD";

        /// <summary>
        /// OPTIONS method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Options = "OPTIONS";

        /// <summary>
        /// PATCH method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Patch = "PATCH";

        /// <summary>
        /// POST method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Post = "POST";

        /// <summary>
        /// PUT method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Put = "PUT";

        /// <summary>
        /// TRACE method.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Trace = "TRACE";

        /// <summary>
        /// Any HTTP method that the instrumentation has no prior knowledge of.
        /// </summary>
        public const string Other = "_OTHER";
    }
}
